side_effects: Catevala fasciata, also known as the Zebra Plant, is generally considered non-toxic to humans and pets. However, ingesting any part of the plant can cause mild gastrointestinal symptoms, such as nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea, if consumed in large quantities. The sap of the plant can cause skin irritation in some individuals, leading to redness and itching. It is essential to handle the plant with care and wear protective gloves when pruning or repotting to minimize potential side effects.
intro: Cactaea fasciata, also known as the Mother of Millions, is a type of succulent plant native to the island of Madagascar, characterized by its distinctive rosette shape and vibrant colors. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and acting as an antimicrobial agent. The bioactive constituents of Catevala fasciata include triterpenoid saponins, flavonoids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Catevala fasciata is often used to prepare herbal remedies such as infusions, tinctures, and extracts, which are used to treat various ailments, including skin conditions, digestive issues, and respiratory problems.
